channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: TMC - Defects -CW6 Potholes problem, 13th May
item.description:

Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Unpermitted roadworks problem, 13th May
item.description:
Customer reports that they currently have a team on site, with all their equipment, who need to replace a bus shelter damaged in an RTC now. Unfortunately, they have overlooked obtaining a signal switch off permit to have the permanent signals switched off so they can install temporary signals. Assistance requested asap, as they need to get he job done.
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: TMC - Defects - Vegetation problem, 13th May
item.description:
Give and take plus temporary footway closure please cut back overgrown hedge and vegetation approx 11 metres x3 meters high (Overgrown Hedge)
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:

item.title: Numerous potholes and surface of road generally in a vey bad condition, 13th May

Whole length of Ermin way has numerous potholes and various areas of the road has deteriorated.

item.title: Unsafe and deteriorating pavement surfaces with repeated failed patch repairs and trip hazards, 13th May
item.description:
Pavement along Clarence Road near the Holst Victorian House Museum / junction area shown on the map pin. The footway surface is heavily deteriorated with widespread cracking, uneven surfacing, failed patch repairs and inconsistent reinstatement materials across an extended section of pavement. There are multiple areas where asphalt patching appears to have broken down or deteriorated around existing paving slabs, creating uneven walking surfaces and potential trip hazards, particularly in wet conditions. The issue appears longstanding rather than isolated, with repeated patch-over-patch repairs visible across the route. Surface condition is poor for pedestrians, wheelchair users, pushchairs and those with mobility impairments. I have photographs and video evidence showing the condition and extent of the deterioration.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Clarence Road, Marle Hill, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Other damage to pavement i.e. cobbles, raised mound/surface

item.title: Unsafe and deteriorating pavement surfaces with repeated failed patch repairs and trip hazards, 13th May
item.description:
Pavement along Clarence Road near the Holst Victorian House Museum / junction area shown on the map pin. The footway surface is heavily deteriorated with widespread cracking, uneven surfacing, failed patch repairs and inconsistent reinstatement materials across an extended section of pavement. There are multiple areas where asphalt patching appears to have broken down or deteriorated around existing paving slabs, creating uneven walking surfaces and potential trip hazards, particularly in wet conditions. The issue appears longstanding rather than isolated, with repeated patch-over-patch repairs visible across the route. Surface condition is poor for pedestrians, wheelchair users, pushchairs and those with mobility impairments. I have photographs and video evidence showing the condition and extent of the deterioration.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 3, Clarence Road, Marle Hill, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The footpath has disappeared and is now a hedge., 13th May
item.description:
From where the footpath enters Highland Road from Southfield Approach, along the head of the cul-de-sac for approx 50m until it reaches the track.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Southern Road, Ewens Farm, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet
